decisions.decision: Export captures the full network state: all markets, all edges (clique-derived + conditional), clique data, and computed layout positions.
alternatives_rejected: Single-market export — rejected because BayesNetGraph fetches and renders the entire network, not a single market's perspective
Omit layout positions — rejected because task description explicitly requests them, even though they're currently deterministic

decisions.decision: Import is visual/read-only — loads a snapshot for viewing in the graph, does not create server-side markets.
alternatives_rejected: Server-side market creation on import — rejected because no bulk creation API exists, createMarket() can't set marginals/cliques, and it would require backend changes far beyond this task's scope

decisions.decision: Add export/import buttons to the existing BayesNetGraph flex header row (between title and stats), not a new toolbar component.
alternatives_rejected: New toolbar component — rejected because the existing header flex row can accommodate buttons with minimal changes, and a separate toolbar adds unnecessary component complexity

decisions.decision: Use hand-rolled TypeScript type guards for import validation, no new validation library.
alternatives_rejected: Add zod/ajv dependency — rejected because the project has zero schema validation libraries, the schema is simple (markets array, edges array, metadata), and ~30-50 lines of type guards suffice

decisions.decision: Edges in export are derived from clique data (via deriveEdgesFromCliques) plus any conditionalEdges if present. On import, edges are loaded directly from the JSON without re-deriving.
alternatives_rejected: Only export clique data and re-derive edges on import — rejected because the export should be a complete snapshot; re-deriving would lose any conditional edges and add unnecessary coupling

steps.task: Define TypeScript types for the network export JSON schema: NetworkExportSchema with version, exportedAt, metadata, nodes (with positions and market data), edges (with source, target, weight, type), and cliques.

Implement export function: serialize current network state (markets, computed positions, derived edges, cliques) into the schema format and trigger a JSON file download via browser API (Blob + URL.createObjectURL + anchor click).

Implement import function: hand-rolled TypeScript validator (type guards for version, nodes array shape, edges array shape), file reading via FileReader, and a callback to surface the parsed NetworkExportSchema to the parent component.

Add export/import UI to BayesNetGraph: insert button group in the header flex row, wire export button to serialize current state, wire import button to a hidden file input that triggers the import flow, add state to toggle between live data and imported snapshot view.

decomposition_assessment.decision_reason: Total estimated lines (280) is well under the 500-line threshold. Zero high-risk steps (below the 2 threshold). While steps 2 and 3 are parallelizable, the groups have sequential dependencies (step1 → [step2, step3] → step4), so there are not 2+ fully independent parallelizable groups. All changes are in a single frontend feature module (graph/) with tightly coupled types. Splitting into children would add coordination overhead without meaningful benefit.
